Remove unused tensorflow from requirements.txt (#167)

TensorFlow was never imported by project code — only listed as a
dependency. It caused noisy startup warnings (oneDNN, CPU flags, absl
logging) on every process launch. TensorBoard (used via PyTorch's
SummaryWriter) does not require TensorFlow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: PWA offline support + class resource tracking

- Full PWA offline support with IndexedDB persistence, offline mutation
  queue, and LWW sync-on-reconnect via syncEngine
- OfflineBanner and InstallPromptBanner components
- NetworkFirst service worker strategy for all /api/* routes
- Zustand character store persisted across sessions
- LAN access: Vite host:true, expanded CORS origins

- ClassResourceEntity, DTOs, migration (AddClassResources)
- ClassResourceSeedService: auto-computes resources per class/level
  (Channel Divinity, Ki Points, Rage, Sorcery Points, Action Surge,
  Second Wind, Bardic Inspiration, Lay on Hands, Pact Magic, etc.)
- ClassResourcesController: use/restore/long-rest/short-rest/sync
- resource_key added to 23 class features in seed JSON
- Frontend: ClassResource type, classResources API module
- StatsPage: Class Resources tracker replaces class features section
  (pip dots, HP pools, sub-options for Channel Divinity choices)
- FeaturesPage: dedicated page for class features + racial traits
- CharacterOverviewPage: Features tile added to nav
- SpellsPerDayPage: Long Rest also resets class resources

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix wavefront marking exit boundary tiles as distance 0 (#166)

When the objective is to exit the room (e.g., move north), the wavefront
was marking ALL boundary tiles as distance 0. This allowed the model to
idle at the boundary moving left/right with 0 PBRS rewards.

Fix: wavefront exit targets are now a single center tile one step
off-screen (e.g., center of north exit at y=-1). This creates a gradient
along the exit boundary — lateral movement away from center produces
negative PBRS, pushing the model to converge on the exit center and leave.

Also fixes dungeon exit reverse mappings in _get_exit_tiles to use the
actual boundary tile coordinates (not the door tile coordinates).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rework Objectives (#165)

* Add game.yaml

* Convert triforce.json to triforce.yaml

Replace JSON config with YAML for easier editing. Update all loaders
in scenario_wrapper.py and models.py to use yaml.safe_load(). Update
references in comments/help text across train.py, diagnose.py,
scenario_selector.py, and tests.

Also adds missing key treasures to game.yaml (rooms 0x53, 0x72) and
wood-sword cave room for 0x77.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add key-aware Dijkstra routing to GameMap

- find_route() uses expanded state (room, keys, collected_mask) for
  key-aware pathfinding through locked doors
- find_next_rooms() returns all equal-cost first moves for multiple
  objective arrows
- find_rooms_with_treasure() for treasure lookup
- Fix backtracking bug: compare full state tuple, not just room
- Add TestTreasureLookup (5 tests) and TestKeyAwareRouting (7 tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ReachLocation and TreasureObjective classes

New objective selectors that use GameMap for routing:
- _GameMapObjective base class with shared room/cave/fight logic
- ReachLocation: routes to a specific (level, location, in_cave)
- TreasureObjective: routes to collect a named treasure
- objective_params plumbing through scenario/state_change wrappers
- RoomMemory now accepts optional GameMap for item lookup
- Fix cave room in game.yaml (add south exit back to overworld)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add end conditions and update scenarios for new objectives

- ReachedLocation: terminates when agent reaches target location
- CollectedTreasure: terminates when target treasure is collected
  (handles triforce, sword, and generic treasure detection)
- End conditions receive objective_params for parameterized scenarios
- Update overworld-sword to use TreasureObjective(treasure=wood-sword)
- Update dungeon1 to use TreasureObjective(treasure=triforce)
- Lint clean (10/10)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert remaining scenarios to new objective types

- skip-sword-to-triforce → TreasureObjective(triforce)
- game-start → TreasureObjective(wood-sword)
- overworld-skip-sword → ReachLocation(dungeon 1 entrance)
- dungeon1-side-room-opening → TreasureObjective(triforce)
- dungeon1-wallmaster → TreasureObjective(triforce)
- dungeon1-skip-opening → TreasureObjective(triforce)
- Only full-game, dungeon1-dungeon2, no-end-conditions remain as
  GameCompletion (multi-objective progression logic deferred)
- Add CollectedTreasure/ReachedLocation end conditions to scenarios
- Update test expectations for new end condition lists

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always fight/collect in dungeon key rooms, track collected keys

Dungeon rooms with treasure always trigger fight/collect objectives,
not just the target room. Key rooms along the path now prompt the
agent to kill enemies and grab the key before proceeding.

Track collected key rooms so Dijkstra routing skips emptied rooms.
Add collected_keys param to GameMap find_route and find_next_rooms.
Fix heap init bug where initial_mask was not propagated.
Add 2 tests for collected_keys routing behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove some end conditions

* Fix wavefront to flow through locked doors when agent has keys

- Fix DOOR_TILES to include all 4 directions (was missing S: 0x9C-0x9F, W: 0xA0-0xA3)
- Add locked_doors parameter to calculate_wavefront_for_link that temporarily
  adds corridor tiles and exit tiles for locked door directions
- Locked door corridors extend 1 tile deeper than open door corridors to cover
  the full 2-tile depth of locked door tiles
- In objectives, add Direction targets when route goes through a locked door
  that isn't in the room's exits
- In state_change_wrapper, detect locked doors and pass to wavefront when agent
  has keys
- Add tests verifying wavefront is blocked by locked doors without keys and
  flows through them with keys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Track opened doors to prevent routing flip-flop

When the agent spends a key to open a locked door, the key count decreases
and the Dijkstra re-routes using the static map which still marks the door
as locked. With 0 keys, the route flips to 'go collect a key' even though
the door is already open.

Fix: _GameMapObjective now detects doors opened at runtime by comparing
the static map (locked) against the actual tile state (is_door_locked).
Opened doors are passed to the Dijkstra which treats them as free passages
(cost 1, no key consumed). This prevents objectives from flip-flopping
when keys are spent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Corrected locked door

* Fix collected key rooms and opened doors in routing

- Clear room_memory.item when room is in _collected_key_rooms to skip
  unnecessary fight/collect logic in already-cleared key rooms
- Pass opened_doors to find_route in TreasureObjective._get_target so
  routing doesn't re-block already-opened doors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix skip-sword-to-triforce scenario to target triforce

Scenario skips the sword cave, so the objective should be triforce
not wood-sword. Removed end conditions that don't apply (sword-specific
and dungeon entry conditions).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add pyyaml to requirements.txt

The yaml module (used by game_map.py for triforce.yaml and game.yaml)
comes from the pyyaml package which was missing from requirements.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor Dijkstra into smaller methods to fix R0915

Extract _build_key_bitmask, _dijkstra, _evaluate_exit, and
_reconstruct_paths from _find_all_routes to reduce statement count.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert GameCompletion to use GameMap-based routing

GameCompletion now extends _GameMapObjective instead of ObjectiveSelector.
Replaces hand-coded room hints and old A* with GameMap Dijkstra routing.
Implements sword priority logic (wood -> white -> magic based on health)
and dynamic triforce targeting (closest uncleared dungeon).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cave transition routing in _get_map_objective

When routing to a target in a cave at the same map location (e.g.,
sword cave at 0x77), get_direction_to returns NONE since the grid
coordinates are identical. Now detects cave transitions (same location,
different in_cave flag) and uses cave_tile as the wavefront target.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use GameCompletion objective for full-game scenario

GameCompletion has sword priority logic — it routes to the sword cave
first, then to triforce pieces. TreasureObjective with treasure:triforce
skips the sword entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove old hand-coded objective routing system

- Delete overworld_to_item, dungeon_to_item, item_to_overworld,
  item_to_dungeon, dungeon_entrances dictionaries
- Delete ObjectiveSelector._get_route_with_astar and
  _enumerate_attached_rooms (replaced by GameMap Dijkstra)
- Move GameMap singleton to ObjectiveSelector so all subclasses
  (including RoomWalk) share it
- Update RoomMemory to always use GameMap for item lookup and
  dungeon entrance detection
- Delete obsolete exploration_objective_analysis.py script
- Remove unused imports (heapq, BoomerangKind, ZeldaItemKind)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: migrate cloud functions from v1 to v2

- Update firebase-functions dependency from ^5.1.0 to ^6.0.0
- Replace functions.pubsub.schedule() with onSchedule() from v2 API
- Keep scheduling intervals at 'every 15 minutes' (both functions)
- No changes to function business logic or database operations
- All environment variables and configuration preserved

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement OpenViking-inspired capabilities (hotness score, used() feedback, L0/L1/L2, fast/quality paths, memory taxonomy, tool stats)

Borrowed 5 high-value capabilities from volcengine/OpenViking, maximally
leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,
rrf_fusion framework already available).

New capabilities:
- Hotness Score: sigmoid(log1p(active_count)) × exp(-λ×age_days) blended
  into RRF fusion at alpha=0.2 (context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py)
- used() feedback API: POST /context/used increments active_count on
  confirmed context items, driving Hotness Score
- MemoryCategory 6-taxonomy: profile/preferences/entities/events/cases/patterns
  with AggregationRequest.category_filter support
- ContextLevel L0/L1/L2: ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW/DETAIL levels with
  AggregationRequest.max_level progressive expansion
- fast/quality dual-path: mode='quality' activates AgenticRetriever
  (openJiuwen-native) for complex tasks; mode='fast' (default) uses
  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries
- Tool Performance Memory: ToolContextGovernor.record_tool_result() +
  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

Files changed:
- context_agent/models/context.py: +ContextLevel, +MemoryCategory enums,
  +ContextItem.{level,category,active_count,updated_at} fields
- context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py: new hotness pure function module
- context_agent/core/retrieval/search_coordinator.py: hotness-blended RRF
- context_agent/core/memory/working_memory.py: +mark_used() method
- context_agent/core/memory/tiered_router.py: +record_usage() method
- context_agent/core/retrieval/tool_governor.py: +record_tool_result(), +_sort_by_success_rate()
- context_agent/adapters/ltm_adapter.py: +agentic_search() with AgenticRetriever fallback
- context_agent/orchestration/context_aggregator.py: +mode/max_level/category_filter params
- context_agent/api/schemas.py: +ContextUsedRequest/Response, +ToolResultRequest/Response
- context_agent/api/http_handler.py: +POST /context/used, +POST /tools/result
- context_agent/api/router.py: +mark_used(), +record_tool_result() facade methods
- tests/unit/test_openviking_capabilities.py: 38 new unit tests (all passing)
- docs/architecture-design.md: +Appendix D with capability details
- docs/agent-integration-guide.md: +Section 9 with usage guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: changelog week of March 9, 2026 (#1103)

Weekly changelog update for the week of March 9, 2026.

Changes included:
- PR #1086 + #1100: Rich text editor with @mentions
- PR #1085: Mobile issue detail page redesign
- PR #1080: Cookie consent banner improvement

Add missing workflow files from OSPO actions

Added standard workflows used across OSPO GitHub Actions:

- auto-labeler.yml: Automatically labels PRs based on release-drafter config
- copilot-setup-steps.yml: Documents Copilot CLI setup for contributors
- linter.yaml: Runs super-linter on PRs to enforce code quality
- pr-title.yml: Validates PR titles follow conventional commit format
- scorecard.yml: Runs OpenSSF Scorecard security analysis
- stale.yaml: Closes stale issues after 35 days of inactivity
- release-drafter.yml: Configuration for auto-labeler and release notes

These workflows provide:
- Automated PR labeling for release notes
- Code quality enforcement via linting
- Security scanning via Scorecard
- Stale issue management
- PR title validation

Brings pr-conflict-detector in line with other OSPO actions like
issue-metrics, stale-repos, contributors, etc.

Copilot/update type hints in files again (#3626)

This pull request modernizes type annotations throughout several metric
modules in the codebase, replacing legacy `typing` constructs (like
`Optional`, `Union`, `List`, and `Tuple`) with Python 3.9+ syntax (e.g.,
`|`, `list`, `tuple`). These changes improve code readability and
maintainability, and ensure consistency across the codebase. No
functional logic is changed—these are strictly type annotation updates.

**Type annotation modernization:**

* Replaced `Optional`, `Union`, `List`, and `Tuple` with Python 3.9+
native type hints (e.g., `str | None`, `list[torch.Tensor]`, `tuple[int,
...]`) in `ignite/metrics/classification_report.py`,
`ignite/metrics/confusion_matrix.py`, `ignite/metrics/epoch_metric.py`,
and `ignite/metrics/mean_average_precision.py`.

commit.message:
fix(ci): reduce API payload size (#14)

* fix(ci): reduce API payload size

Evaluate call (~44KB -> ~26KB):
- Remove README.md from codebase context (12KB of user docs adds
  no value for classification/planning)
- Halve the char cap from 60k to 20k chars (~5k tokens)

Implement call (~44KB -> ~3-8KB typically):
- Load only ev.plan.affected_files instead of the full codebase;
  the evaluation already identified which files need changes
- Falls back to all Lua files if affected_files is empty/unreadable
- Cap raised to 40k chars since this call needs richer context

commit.message:
feat(ci): automated issue triage and implementation workflow (#9)

Adds .github/workflows/issue-triage.yml which triggers on every new
issue opened. The workflow uses the GitHub Models API (o3-mini by default,
configurable via GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var) to:

- Classify the issue as bug, enhancement, or other and apply the
  corresponding label automatically
- Score difficulty (1-5) and impact (1-5) with short reasoning
- Route to one of three outcomes:
  * impact < 4 AND type=bug or type=enhancement  → label 'evaluation needed' + post evaluation summary
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty >= 4 → post a structured plan comment
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty <= 3 → generate code, push a branch, open PR
  * type=other AND any impact  -> label 'evaluation needed'

No additional secrets are required — GITHUB_TOKEN covers both the
GitHub Models API and PR creation. The model can be swapped by changing
the GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var (e.g. gpt-4o, o1, gpt-4o-mini).

Allows the issue triage workflow to be run manually from the GitHub
Actions UI for any existing issue, in addition to triggering
automatically on newly opened issues.

Replace the static sourceFiles array in both the evaluate and
auto-implement steps with a recursive fs.readdirSync('lua') call.
This keeps codebase context up to date automatically as new Lua
modules are added without requiring workflow changes.

Previously only impact < 4 triggered the label. Now type=other always
gets 'evaluation needed' regardless of impact score, matching the
intended behaviour that unclassified issues always need manual review.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix `open` insertion position for nested explicit modules (issue #789) (#1495)

* Fix open insertion to place inside nearest enclosing module (issue #789)

FCS's TryFindInsertionContext sets Pos.Line to the module *declaration*
line (e.g. 'module Foo =') rather than the first line inside the module.
adjustInsertionPoint passed this value straight through for ScopeKind.Module,
which caused the generated 'open' to be inserted *before* the module
declaration instead of inside it.

Fix: detect when the candidate insertion line is an explicit module
declaration ('module X =') and advance by one so the open lands inside
the module.  This makes the code-fix behaviour consistent with code
completion (issue #789).

Also adds isExplicitModuleDeclaration helper and a regression test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

commit.message:
Fix rendering efficiency and screencopy Y-flip

Rendering: Skip the full bind/clear/draw/finish/submit cycle when
nothing visual has changed (no new Electron frames, no dirty Wayland
surfaces, no cursor movement). In the DRM path, also skip when no
screencopy or portal streams are active. Frame callbacks are always
sent unconditionally to prevent Wayland client stalls.

Screencopy: Remove the incorrect row-flip after glReadPixels in both
the wlr-screencopy fulfillment and portal stream readback paths.
Smithay already applies a Y-correction when rendering to GBM-backed
FBOs, so glReadPixels returns top-down data. The manual flip was
inverting an already-correct image, producing upside-down output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test execution (#114)

* Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test support

Implement thread-local dispatch for ARM32 architecture, matching the
existing x86_64 and aarch64 implementations. This enables parallel test
execution on ARM32 Linux targets by replacing the global mutex approach
with per-thread function replacement routing.

Changes:
- Add target_arch = "arm" to all thread-local dispatch cfg gates across
  thread_local_registry.rs, internal.rs, injector.rs, common.rs, and
  thread_safety.rs
- Implement ARM32 dispatcher JIT code generation (ARM mode) with
  register save/restore for both integer (r0-r3) and VFP (d0-d7) regs
- Add ARM32 trampoline creation with support for both ARM and Thumb mode
  jump-back sequences
- Add PC-relative instruction fixup for ARM mode trampolines
- Add ARM32 branch patch generation matching existing patch_arm.rs format
  including Thumb alignment handling
- Add ARM32 JIT code for boolean return values (MOV r0, #imm; BX lr)
- Enable allocate_jit_memory for ARM32 (uses simple mmap fallback)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ARM32 dispatcher stack alignment and patch register clobbering

- Fix AAPCS stack alignment violation: PUSH {r0-r3, r4, lr} (24 bytes,
  8-aligned) instead of PUSH {r0-r3, lr} (20 bytes, misaligned).
  Misaligned SP before BLX caused SIGABRT on armv7.

- Use r12 (IP, scratch register) instead of r7 (callee-saved/frame pointer)
  for Thumb branch patch. r7 clobbering corrupted the caller's frame pointer,
  causing SIGSEGV on thumbv7. Uses MOVW/MOVT r12 encoding which is also
  alignment-independent.

- Use r12 instead of r9 (callee-saved) for ARM branch patch.

* Reduce ARM32 test stress and add diagnostics for CI

- Add RUST_BACKTRACE=full for ARM target CI runs
- Run ARM tests with --test-threads=1 to avoid resource exhaustion
  on 32-bit compat runners (armv7 was hitting thread spawn limits)
- Reduce stress test THREAD_COUNT from 20 to 4 on ARM32

* Fix ARM32 function overflow with dynamic 4-byte patch size

Use dynamic patch sizing for ARM32: 4-byte B.W (Thumb) or B (ARM) when
the dispatcher is within branch range (±16MB/±32MB), falling back to
12-byte MOVW/MOVT/BX when it is not. This eliminates the previous
12-byte patch overflow into adjacent small functions that caused
SIGSEGV on thumbv7.

Key changes:
- Add ARM32 to constrained JIT allocation (±16MB range) in common.rs
- Pre-allocate dispatcher, then choose patch size based on distance
- For Thumb 4-byte patches, prepend a Thumb-to-ARM stub (LDR.W/BX)
  since Thumb B.W cannot switch processor mode
- Encode Thumb B.W (T4) and ARM B (unconditional) for 4-byte patches
- Add -j 1 to CI cargo test for ARM targets to prevent resource
  exhaustion from parallel test binary execution

* Add diagnostics for thumbv7 SIGSEGV in test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread

Add eprintln diagnostics to:
- install_dispatcher_arm32: log addresses, distance, patch size
- generate_branch_patch_arm32: verify B.W encoding round-trips correctly
- test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread: log each step to identify crash point

Also add isolated test run CI step for ARM targets to determine if the
crash is caused by accumulated state from prior tests.

* Fix ARM32 Thumb overlapping patch SIGILL and instruction boundary bugs

- Use std::hint::black_box in test helper functions to ensure minimum
  4-byte code size on Thumb (prevents compiler from generating 2-byte
  functions that cause overlapping patches)
- Add calculate_thumb_copy_size() to scan Thumb instructions and find
  the first complete instruction boundary >= patch_size bytes, preventing
  the trampoline from splitting a 32-bit Thumb instruction
- Add check_arm32_patch_overlap() to detect overlapping patches at
  runtime with clear diagnostic messages (only checks active patches
  with ref_count > 0)
- Remove diagnostic eprintln statements and isolated test CI step

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Filter out conflicts where both PRs have the same author

If a developer has two of their own PRs conflicting, they likely
already know about it and don't need a Slack notification.

Changes:
- Added same-author filtering after conflict detection
- Filters out conflicts where pr_a.author == pr_b.author
- Prints count of filtered conflicts when > 0
- Added test to verify filtering behavior

Example output:
  'Filtered 3 same-author conflict(s) (12 remaining)'

Test status: 178 tests passing, 100% coverage on orchestrator, 9.99/10 pylint

commit.message:
Fix state file persistence and dry run behavior

- Remove .pr-conflict-state.json from .gitignore
  - State file now commits to repo for persistence between runs
  - Fixes issue where deduplication state was lost each run

- Update dry run mode to skip state file saving
  - DRY_RUN=true now prevents state file modifications
  - Allows testing without affecting deduplication state
  - Added test to verify save_state is not called in dry run

Test status: 177 tests passing, 9.99/10 pylint
